key_insight: Machine-learning interatomic potentials can give a more internally consistent and accurate picture of phonon transport in bulk and nanocrystalline silicon than traditional empirical potentials.
engineering_relevance: This enables better prediction of thermal conductivity and grain-boundary thermal resistance for designing nanoscale electronic and nanomechanical devices.
method: Train and use GAP and MACE machine-learning interatomic potentials for silicon.
Derive harmonic and anharmonic force constants with a unified Phonopy/Phono3py workflow.
Run NEMD to quantify grain-boundary thermal boundary resistance in nanocrystalline silicon.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparisons against Stillinger-Weber and Tersoff potentials, with the MLIPs producing more accurate bulk phonons and interfacial heat-transport predictions.
watch_item: Results still depend on the quality and transferability of the ML potential and on how well the grain-boundary structure and roughness are represented in simulation.
paper_title: Phonon-Mediated Thermal Transport in Nanocrystalline Silicon Using Machine-Learning Interatomic Potentials
